tasks_completed.description: Item D — Tightened PreTool read_grant literal-match to exact-only, closing PreTool/PostTool asymmetry that allowed substring grants (e.g. /allow Re) to bypass PreTool gates while never being consumed at PostTool
tasks_completed.changes: Added third literal_policy enum branch 'exact_only' to _match_loaded_grant (line 88-89). Switched read_grant's _load_and_match call from 'exact_or_substr' to 'exact_only' (line 144). Updated read_grant docstring with cycle 20260519-211515 Item D rationale (line 131-141). PostTool consume_grant_for_posttool Branch 3 (already exact-only) unchanged. read_grant_for_git_command (substr_only) unchanged. Regex grants unchanged.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause per BA spec Evidence section: read_grant passed 'exact_or_substr' literal_policy → pattern 'Re' matched 'Read' via substring → grant returned True at PreTool. PostTool Branch 3 hard-coded exact equality → 'Re' != 'Read' → grant NOT unlinked → leakage past single-use. Fix mirrors PostTool semantics symmetrically.
tasks_completed.verification: AC-D1 smoke: read_grant('Read', sid) returns False for pattern 'Re'. AC-D2: pattern 'Read' returns True; consume_grant_for_posttool unlinks. AC-D3: 6/6 production callers verified. AC-D4: regex grants unchanged. AC-D-H: prior cycle's read-size-guard + block-enterworktree bypass paths still work with exact-match grant bodies.

tasks_completed.ac: AC-H1, AC-H2, AC-H3a, AC-H3b (all 4 sub-cases), AC-H4
tasks_completed.description: Item H — Replaced bookmark-in_progress inference with bounded-window K=3 first-unflipped-match keyed on subagent_type, gated on a single valid in_progress anchor. Mutually-exclusive Case A / Case B. None-key guard. Multi-in_progress guard.
tasks_completed.changes: Added multi-in_progress guard to _current_in_progress_index (returns None when >1 in_progress found). Added new helper _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step (K=3 bounded window, returns None when ip_index is None or no unflipped match, handles both dict and list-shaped subagent_call). Added _extract_subagent_type helper (normalizes stdin.tool_input.subagent_type). Split _resolve_context (strict — preserved for Path A contract path) from new _resolve_base_context (no subagent_call gate — needed by Case A typed lookup). Restructured _main: load base context once, check contract presence FIRST (per codex Finding 1 — contract-present sessions route strictly through Path A's _enforce, never legacy write), then mutually-exclusive Case A (subagent_type non-empty → typed lookup only, NO fall-through to Case B) / Case B (subagent_type absent → legacy chain). None-key guard via 'target is not None and isinstance(target, int)' before _record_subagent_call_legacy.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause per BA spec Evidence section: PostToolUse:Agent inferred which canonical step to flip from bookmark in_progress; parallel TodoWrite (Step N→in_progress) + Agent dispatch in one response left the bookmark stale, so the hook either flipped the wrong step or no-wrote. Fix: when subagent_type is in stdin, scan a K=3 window starting at ip_index for the first unflipped step matching that type. Gated on single valid anchor.
tasks_completed.verification: AC-H1: parallel race writes target step (ip=2, target=3). AC-H2: serial writes ip step. AC-H3a: subagent_type absent + legacy resolves → Case B writes. AC-H3b sub-cases (1)(2)(3)(4) all no-write (no None key, no wrong-step flip, no canonical[0:3] scan when ip is None/multi). AC-H4 regression: git diff confirms pretool-todo-validate.py:251-270 Gate 4 untouched.

tasks_completed.description: Added regression tests for Item D
tasks_completed.changes: Added 2 tests in TestReadGrant class: test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_substring (pattern 'Re' MUST NOT match tool 'Read'; grant file remains in place) and test_read_grant_exact_only_rejects_write_against_todowrite (pattern 'Write' MUST NOT match tool 'TodoWrite' — exact mirror of the original PreTool/PostTool asymmetry).
tasks_completed.rationale: AC-D1 mandates a regression test. Both tests fail under the pre-cycle exact_or_substr semantics and pass under the new exact_only semantics, locking the fix into CI.

git_rationale.root_cause_commits: D: pre-existing condition since hooks/lib/allowlist.py creation (allow-6 consolidation, task 20260518-155948). H: PostToolUse:Agent + bookmark inference architecture predates the parallel-dispatch use case introduced later.
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: D: consolidation extracted PreTool semantics as 'exact_or_substr' while PostTool semantics evolved to 'exact_only' (Branch 3 docstring at lib/allowlist.py:239-241) without reconciling the asymmetry. H: bookmark inference assumed serial TodoWrite-then-Agent emission shape; orchestrator's parallel dispatch breaks the assumption.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: D: align PreTool literal match with PostTool's documented exact-only contract (Branch 3 docstring). H: replace inferred-target with deterministic subagent_type keyed lookup, gated on single valid anchor.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Item H's diff size is mandated by the BA ticket's explicit Case A/Case B mutually-exclusive split requirement (Technical Hints section). The new helper alone is ~50 LOC; the Case A/Case B refactor of _main is ~70 LOC; multi-in_progress guard + list-shape normalization (codex Finding 2) + contract-presence reordering (codex Finding 1) add another ~50 LOC of structural code. Each AC (H1, H2, H3a, H3b sub-cases, contract-bypass) requires a distinct code path; cannot be collapsed without violating AC-H3b's 'NO fall-through' mandate. Item D is ~20 lines (small).

dev.qa_notes: Run pytest hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py (27 tests, all pass). For Item H, an in-process harness is provided at /var/tmp/dev-test/test_h.py covering 10 scenarios: AC-H1, AC-H2, AC-H3a, AC-H3b(1-4), list-shape (Finding 2), contract-blocks-legacy (Finding 1), contract-routes-to-enforce. AC-D3: 6 production callers (orchestrator-gate.py:184, subagent-enforce.py:214, write-guard.sh:39, aggregate-check.py:389, read-size-guard.py:72, block-enterworktree.sh:48) verified pass with exact patterns and reject with substring patterns.

tests: AC-D3 smoke: 6/6 production callers verified (exact True, substring False)
AC-D-H smoke: prior cycle's /allow Read + /allow EnterWorktree bypass paths still work
blast_radius_declarations.high_impact_reason: Touched by 8+ production hooks. Behavior change is strictly tightening (exact_only instead of exact_or_substr) — substring grants that previously matched now reject. Existing /allow grants stored by users are unaffected (user-typed grant patterns are documented to be canonical tool names).

imported_by: Triggered as PostToolUse:Agent hook by settings.json hook registration; no Python imports.
tests: /var/tmp/dev-test/test_h.py — 10/10 pass (AC-H1, AC-H2, AC-H3a, AC-H3b(1-4), list-shape, contract-blocks-legacy, contract-routes-enforce)

self_verification.notes: All Python files syntax-check via ast.parse. pytest 27/27. AC-D3 6/6 production callers. AC-D-H prior cycle bypass paths. 10/10 H smoke tests (including 2 codex Finding-fix regression tests).

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 3 findings: (1) BLOCKER — contract-present /dev-overnight sessions could bypass contract reconciliation via legacy Case A write when strict _resolve_context returned None. (2) MAJOR — typed lookup helper only handled dict-shaped subagent_call, regressing list-shape support that pretool-todo-validate.py:244-248 expects. (3) BLOCKER_FOR_CLOSE_DEBATE / OBSERVATION_ONLY — on-disk docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md is the parallel cycle's harness spec (3-Cluster Harness Fixes) not D+H content; ticket file collision per context.collision_report_recurring. Findings 1+2 are in_scope_real_bug — fixed in this cycle. Finding 3 is out_of_scope for dev (orchestrator-side collision recovery).
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 fixed by restructuring _main to load base context once, then check _try_load_contract BEFORE Case A/B. Contract-present sessions now route strictly through Path A's _enforce path (with single-ip + subagent_call gate); never fall through to legacy write. Finding 2 fixed by normalizing _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step's subagent_call handling to accept both dict and list-of-dicts shapes (mirroring pretool-todo-validate.py:_format_subagent_names). Added 2 regression tests: test_contract_blocks_legacy_write, test_contract_routes_to_enforce_on_strict_match, test_list_shape_subagent_call (in /var/tmp/dev-test/test_h.py — see qa_notes). Finding 3 surfaced to orchestrator in this report (see ticket_collision_observation below); dev did NOT modify the ticket file per dispatch prompt instructions (collision recovery is orchestrator-side).

findings_detail.title: contract-present sessions could bypass contract reconciliation
findings_detail.evidence: Original _main called strict _resolve_context first; if it returned None (e.g., bookmark's ip lacks subagent_call), fell through to legacy Case A typed lookup without checking _try_load_contract — bypassing artifact validation.
findings_detail.fix: Restructured _main: load _resolve_base_context once, call _try_load_contract immediately. If contract present, strict Path A only (single ip + subagent_call → _enforce, otherwise no-write). Case A/B only run when contract absent.

findings_detail.title: list-shaped subagent_call regression
findings_detail.evidence: pretool-todo-validate.py:244-248 supports subagent_call as a list (multiple alternative subagents); original _find_earliest_unflipped_matching_step only handled dict shape, would silently miss list-shape canonical metadata.
findings_detail.fix: Normalized helper to accept dict OR list-of-dicts; matches if any entry's subagent_type equals needle.

findings_detail.title: on-disk ticket file contains parallel-cycle harness spec, not D+H
findings_detail.evidence: docs/dev/ticket-20260519-211515.md line 1 says '3-Cluster Harness Fixes'; line 8 self-acknowledges D+H content was superseded by harness work. Same collision is recorded in context.collision_report_recurring as the recurring task-id allocator bug.
findings_detail.fix_responsibility: orchestrator-side (per dispatch prompt: 'Two ghost overwrites of the canonical task-id slot occurred... they are NOT your concern'). Dev did NOT modify the ticket file. The authoritative D+H content for this cycle is the context JSON + acceptance-criteria JSON + dispatch prompt's explicit file/AC enumeration.

recommendations: Orchestrator: resolve the recurring task-id slot collision (per context.collision_report_recurring) — second collision in same slot indicates allocator bug; ticket-20260519-211515.md on disk is parallel cycle's harness spec, NOT the D+H spec implemented this cycle.

Consider adding the in-process H harness (currently /var/tmp/dev-test/test_h.py) as a permanent pytest under hooks/tests/test_posttool_subagent_track.py in a follow-up cycle — would lock the Case A/B contract presence routing into CI alongside the allowlist tests.

If the validator's list-shape subagent_call support is intended to be deprecated, consider documenting it explicitly and converging on dict-only. If retained, document the case in a new docstring example.
